4.1.name: Write failing Tier A unit tests for StateManager.ts

1.evidence: PASS — root aggregator file __tests__/StateManager.test.ts present; imports 17 nested test files under __tests__/StateManager/. 20 fixture JSON files under __tests__/fixtures/. Stub src/StateManager.ts (111 lines) and src/lib/state-types.ts (55 lines) also created to let TypeScript resolve imports during red phase.

2.check: Test file runs and currently FAILS (red phase)
2.command: cd ~/projects/dev/dev-tools/agentics-dev/tools-dev/plan-executor-tools-dev && bun test __tests__/StateManager.test.ts > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo FAIL || echo PASS
2.evidence: PASS — bun test exits 1 (suite fails). Final tally: 59 fail / 8 pass / 68 expect() calls across 67 tests in 1 aggregated file. The 8 passes are legitimate export-existence smoke tests in exports.test.ts (TA-StateManager-041/042/043) that verify the function/error-class exports exist as callable symbols — these will remain valid after green phase with minor expected-message updates.

3.check: Test coverage maps to StateManager-related FR/TR entries in test-plan.md
3.prompt: Did you verify that each test case in StateManager.test.ts maps to a specific FR-X.X or TR-X.X from test-plan.md?
3.evidence: Verified 2026-04-21T23:35:00Z. Each test description carries its TA-StateManager-NNN ID plus the verifying FR-X.X / TR-X.X IDs, matching test-plan.md §3.1. Honest disclosure: the first delegation (23 min, Sonnet, stalled on stream watchdog) produced tests with an anti-TDD pattern — 59 of 67 bodies asserted 'expect(() => fn()).toThrow("not implemented")' which is tautologically true against the stub. A cleanup delegation (10 min, Sonnet) rewrote those 59 assertions to real behavioral checks. 8 of the rewrites required synthesising assertions where the first agent's 'Phase 4.2 will implement:' comment was incomplete: TA-StateManager-010, -024, -030, -031, -032, -033, -034, -035, -049, TA-Checksum-002, TA-Checksum-004. Three of those (TA-030/031/032 and TA-033/034) synthesised a third optional 'path' parameter to advanceTask() that Phase 4.2 implementation must honour — decision leaked from tests. Post-mortem captured in session notes: single-shot mass delegation + no fail-fast TDD quality gate were the root causes; subsequent phases should use orchestrator-direct implementation, smaller sub-delegations, or a quality checkpoint after every ~5 tests.

8.5.name: Smoke test — canned validation.json under hook enforcement

1.evidence: Full 6-step smoke test at /tmp/smoke-test/ with 1-phase 1-task 1-automated-criterion plan. Results: (1) init writes pointer + checksum sha256:1f6c62... (2) Write attempt BLOCKED with permissionDecision=deny + correct reason mentioning task id + remediation command (3) Bash with ~/.claude/PAI/Tools/CheckRunner.ts tilde path → silent ALLOW (allow-list via realpath after tilde expansion) (4) CheckRunner without marker → FAIL with exit_code=0 (classifier detected FAIL on stdout despite exit 0) (5) marker created + CheckRunner re-run → FAIL criterion re-evaluated to PASS → advanceTask → plan COMPLETED → pointer deleted (6) Write attempt after pointer deletion → silent ALLOW. Event log at /tmp/smoke-test/.plan-executor/events.jsonl contains all 5 expected event types: plan.gate.blocked, plan.gate.allowed, plan.criterion.failed, plan.criterion.passed, plan.task.advanced (with plan_completed=true). TWO BUGS CAUGHT AND FIXED: (a) allow-list tokeniser did not expand ~ to $HOME — added expandHome() in PlanGateHandler.ts; (b) CheckRunner skipped FAIL criteria on re-run (guard was !== PENDING; changed to === PASS). Both bugs have new regression tests (TA-PlanGate-019b, TA-CheckRunner-025b). Full suite: 126/126 pass.
